# Requirements Quality Checklist: UC-20 View Course History

**Purpose**: Validate the UC-20 requirements for completeness, clarity, consistency, measurability, and scenario coverage before task breakdown and implementation
**Created**: 2026-03-11
**Feature**: [spec.md](/home/thaia/ECE493Group19/specs/020-branch-course-history/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are requirements defined for every student-visible course-history state: complete, partial, empty, and denied access? [Completeness, Spec §User Stories 1-3; Spec §FR-005; Spec §FR-005a; Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK002 Does the spec define what information a "course history" entry must contain beyond academic period and final outcome? [Gap, Spec §FR-002; Spec §FR-003; Spec §Key Entities]
- [x] CHK003 Are authorization requirements complete for both dashboard navigation and direct-link or manipulated-path access attempts? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001; Spec §FR-004; Spec §FR-006; Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK004 Are requirements specified for how missing or unavailable history data should be identified to the student in all partial-history cases? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005; Spec §User Story 2]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK005 Is "readable format" defined with criteria that can be objectively reviewed rather than interpreted differently by different implementers? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-003]
- [x] CHK006 Is "equivalent student navigation" sufficiently specific to avoid multiple incompatible entry-point interpretations? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-001]
- [x] CHK007 Is "authorized student" defined clearly enough to distinguish self-service access from other possible academic roles or proxy access patterns? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004; Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK008 Is "some information is missing" defined with explicit messaging or indicator requirements rather than general wording? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-005; Spec §SC-002]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK009 Do the user stories, edge cases, and functional requirements use the same definition of course history as "all past enrollments with final outcomes" without reintroducing narrower wording? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications; Spec §User Story 1; Spec §FR-002]
- [x] CHK010 Are the empty-history requirements clearly distinct from partial-history requirements everywhere the spec describes missing data conditions? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications; Spec §Edge Cases; Spec §FR-005; Spec §FR-005a]
- [x] CHK011 Do the success criteria align with the functional requirements for denied access logging, rather than leaving audit expectations implied only in one section? [Consistency, Spec §FR-007; Spec §SC-003]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK012 Can each success criterion be evaluated objectively without inventing additional rules during planning or implementation? [Acceptance Criteria, Measurability, Spec §SC-001 to §SC-005]
- [x] CHK013 Is the 30-second dashboard-to-history outcome tied to a clearly defined start point and completion point? [Clarity, Measurability, Spec §SC-004]
- [x] CHK014 Is "representative students" in the comprehension outcome defined well enough to avoid subjective sampling during validation? [Ambiguity, Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-005]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK015 Are primary, alternate, and exception scenarios all covered by explicit requirements, not only by narrative user stories? [Coverage, Spec §User Stories 1-3; Spec §FR-001 to §FR-008]
- [x] CHK016 Are repeat-view scenarios intentionally covered as read-only behavior, including whether results must remain stable across repeated access in one session? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases; Spec §FR-008]
- [x] CHK017 Does the spec define whether unauthorized attempts against another student's history and unauthorized attempts caused by session-state mismatch are treated the same way? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §User Story 3; Spec §FR-006; Spec §FR-007]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK018 Are requirements defined for the case where course-history records exist but critical fields for display are missing or incomplete? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §FR-005; Spec §Key Entities]
- [x] CHK019 Are boundary conditions defined for students with very large course histories spanning many academic periods? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §FR-002; Plan §Scale/Scope]
- [x] CHK020 Does the spec distinguish "no history exists" from "history retrieval failed completely" so implementers do not collapse different states into one response? [Edge Case, Conflict, Spec §FR-005; Spec §FR-005a; Spec §Edge Cases]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK021 Are accessibility requirements for keyboard navigation and assistive-technology announcements explicitly stated in the spec rather than only implied in the plan or contract artifacts? [Gap, Spec §Requirements; Plan §Phase 1 Design & Contracts]
- [x] CHK022 Are privacy expectations for course-history data exposure defined explicitly enough to guide reviewer checks beyond basic authorization wording? [Coverage, Spec §FR-004; Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK023 Are responsiveness requirements complete for both normal page loading and degraded data-availability conditions? [Completeness, Spec §SC-004; Plan §Technical Context]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK024 Are assumptions about existing student identity, authorization status, and academic record sources documented in a way that can be validated during planning? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions; Spec §Dependencies]
- [x] CHK025 Does the spec define what happens if a dependency needed for authorization-failure logging is unavailable when access must still be denied? [Gap, Dependency, Spec §Dependencies; Spec §FR-006; Spec §FR-007]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK026 Is there any conflict between "course history" as a broad record of past enrollments and possible transcript-style interpretations left elsewhere in the spec or source artifacts? [Conflict, Spec §Clarifications; Spec §FR-002]
- [x] CHK027 Are terms such as "course history," "final outcome," "missing," and "authorized" defined consistently enough to avoid different interpretations in tasks and acceptance evidence? [Consistency, Traceability, Spec §Clarifications; Spec §Requirements; Spec §Key Entities]
- [x] CHK028 Does the spec explicitly exclude out-of-scope viewer roles or editing capabilities so planners do not infer extra features? [Gap, Scope, Spec §Assumptions; Spec §FR-008]

## Contract & Data Model Alignment

- [x] CHK029 Are the data elements implied by the UI contract and data model also required in the spec, rather than only documented in design artifacts? [Traceability, Gap, Spec §FR-002; Spec §FR-003; Data Model §Course History Record; Contract §Complete History View]
- [x] CHK030 Are requirements clear about which record attributes are mandatory for display versus optional when data is partial? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005; Spec §Key Entities; Data Model §Course History Record]
- [x] CHK031 Do the spec and UI contract align on the difference between denied access, empty history, and partial data without introducing contradictory display expectations? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005; Spec §FR-005a; Spec §FR-006; Contract §View States]
- [x] CHK032 Is the authorization-failure record required with enough detail in the spec to support objective review of what must be captured? [Completeness, Spec §FR-007; Spec §Key Entities; Data Model §Authorization Failure Record]

## Recovery & Failure Handling

- [x] CHK033 Are recovery requirements defined for temporary academic-record source outages, or is their absence an intentional scope decision? [Gap, Recovery, Spec §Dependencies; Spec §FR-005]
- [x] CHK034 Does the spec define what the student should see when no course-history data can be retrieved at all, as distinct from partial history and true empty history? [Coverage, Exception Flow, Spec §FR-005; Spec §FR-005a; Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK035 Are audit/logging requirements complete for failure-to-log scenarios, including whether denial must still proceed if audit recording is degraded? [Dependency, Exception Flow, Spec §FR-006; Spec §FR-007; Spec §Dependencies]

## Traceability Quality

- [x] CHK036 Is there a clear one-to-one traceability path from each major requirement cluster to user stories, success criteria, and acceptance artifacts? [Traceability, Spec §User Stories 1-3; Spec §FR-001 to §FR-009; Spec §SC-001 to §SC-005]
- [x] CHK037 Are clarifications incorporated into normative requirements, not left only in the clarification log where they could be missed during planning? [Consistency, Traceability, Spec §Clarifications; Spec §FR-002; Spec §FR-005a]
- [x] CHK038 Do plan-level expectations for accessibility, performance, and validation reveal any requirement gaps that should be promoted back into the spec? [Gap, Traceability, Plan §Technical Context; Plan §Phase 1 Design & Contracts]

## Validation Readiness

- [x] CHK039 Are the requirement IDs and success criteria specific enough that each quickstart scenario can be traced back to a written requirement without inventing missing links? [Traceability, Spec §FR-001 to §FR-009; Spec §SC-001 to §SC-005; Quickstart §Validation Scenarios]
- [x] CHK040 Does the spec define enough about "direct route" or alternate access paths for unauthorized access coverage to be reviewed consistently? [Clarity, Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases; Quickstart §4]
- [x] CHK041 Are the requirements explicit about what constitutes a "final outcome" so reviewers can tell whether withdrawn, failed, transferred, or equivalent statuses belong in scope? [Clarity, Gap, Spec §Clarifications; Spec §FR-002; Spec §Key Entities]

## State Definition Quality

- [x] CHK042 Are complete, partial, empty, and denied states defined as requirement-level concepts rather than only as design-contract labels? [Completeness, Traceability, Spec §FR-005; Spec §FR-005a; Spec §FR-006; Contract §View States]
- [x] CHK043 Does the spec define whether a student can ever see mixed states across terms, such as some terms complete and others entirely unavailable, without ambiguity? [Edge Case, Clarity, Spec §FR-005; Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK044 Are requirements clear on whether the missing-data indicator must be global for the page, attached to specific terms, or both? [Clarity, Gap, Spec §FR-005; Spec §User Story 2]

## Assumption Risk

- [x] CHK045 Are assumptions about pre-existing academic record sources and authorization services framed as assumptions to validate, rather than hidden requirements that could fail later planning? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions; Spec §Dependencies]
- [x] CHK046 Does the spec define whether account states that permit sign-in but restrict course-history visibility require different handling from outright unauthorized access? [Coverage, Ambiguity, Spec §Assumptions; Spec §FR-004; Spec §FR-006]

## Notes

- Focus areas applied: authorization/privacy and UX/scenario coverage, with standard spec-wide review depth.
- Intended primary user: PR reviewer.
- This checklist tests the quality of the written requirements, not implementation behavior.
- Review completed on 2026-03-11 against the current `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `data-model.md`, `course-history-ui-contract.md`, and `quickstart.md`.
- Currently satisfied: CHK001 through CHK046.
- No material requirement-quality gaps remain in the current specification based on this checklist pass.
